purpose: Allow a Sustainability Consultant to submit generated project documents for human review, start review processing, and record approve or reject decisions with full auditability before downstream packaging or submission use.
trigger: The user selects a generated document on the Documents screen and submits it for review or records a review decision using api-documents-review.
preconditions: The user is authenticated and tenant context is resolved.
The user has project-scoped permission to manage narratives or document reviews.
The project exists and is accessible to the actor under RBAC scope rules.
The target document has already been generated and stored as a document artifact.
The document belongs to the specified project.
The document is in a mutable review state and has not been superseded by a newer immutable package context.
Required template and branding references for the document version are already pinned on generation.
inputSource: Documents screen scr-documents via POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/documents/{documentId}/review with authenticated session token, X-Tenant-Id header, Idempotency-Key header, and If-Match header.
output: Updated document review status on the target document artifact, persisted document review event history, audit trail entry, and API response containing the updated document metadata.
mainFlow: User opens the Documents screen and selects a generated document for review action.
System validates authentication, tenant context, project scope access, and permission to review generated documents.
System loads the project and target document artifact using projectId and documentId.
System verifies that the document belongs to the specified project and is eligible for the requested action.
If action is submit, system changes review status from draft to inReview and records a document review event.
If action is startReview, system records review-start intent and keeps the document in inReview state.
If action is approve, system updates the document review status to approved, records reviewer identity and review timestamp, and creates a document review event.
If action is reject, system updates the document review status to rejected, records reviewer identity and review timestamp, and creates a document review event with comments.
System writes an immutable audit log describing the state change, actor, target resource, correlation context, and before and after values.
System returns the updated document artifact to the caller.
System makes the updated review state available for subsequent package-building and document-list refresh operations.
alternativeFlow: User submits a document that is already inReview; system treats the request idempotently and returns the current inReview document state.
User starts review on a document already inReview; system records the action idempotently or returns current state without duplicate status transition.
User approves a document that was previously rejected after a regenerated review cycle; system accepts the new decision only if the document remains mutable and current.
User rejects a document with explanatory comments; system stores the rejection comments and leaves the document available for regeneration or correction workflow.
A repeated request arrives with the same idempotency key; system returns the previously committed outcome without duplicating events.
An Admin with equivalent scoped permissions performs the action on behalf of the consultant under RBAC; system records the actual acting user in the audit trail.
exceptionFlow: If authentication fails or tenant header is missing, system rejects the request and no review state is changed.
If the actor lacks project-scoped permission for document review, system denies the action and records an authorization failure audit event.
If projectId or documentId does not resolve to an existing accessible resource, system returns not found and no state is changed.
If the document does not belong to the specified project, system rejects the request as an invalid resource association.
If action is not one of submit, startReview, approve, or reject, system rejects the request as invalid input.
If an approve or reject action is attempted for a document that is not in a valid reviewable state, system rejects the transition.
If optimistic concurrency or duplicate processing safeguards detect a conflicting update, system returns a conflict-safe failure and preserves the last committed state.
If persistence of the document status update, review event, or audit log fails, system rolls back the transaction and returns an error.
If downstream audit logging is unavailable, system fails the operation rather than committing an unaudited sensitive state transition.
validationRules: projectId must be a valid non-empty identifier.
documentId must be a valid non-empty identifier.
action must be one of submit, startReview, approve, or reject.
comments may be empty for submit or startReview but must not exceed configured maximum length.
comments should be provided for reject decisions to support human correction workflow traceability.
idempotencyKey is required for the review POST operation.
ifMatch must contain the current etag of the mutable DocumentArtifact being updated.
The referenced document must exist in content.DocumentArtifact.
The referenced project must exist in project.Project.
The document's projectId must equal the route projectId.
The document review status transition must comply with allowed state rules.
businessRules: Document review is a human-in-the-loop control point before downstream submission usage.
Only users with appropriate RBAC permissions and project scope may submit or decide document reviews.
Review decisions must be fully auditable with actor, time, and comments where applicable.
Approved documents are eligible for downstream packaging and submission workflows.
Rejected documents remain traceable and may require regeneration or correction before reuse.
Normal review actions do not require dual approval.
Document review history is append-only through review events and separate from mutable current document status.
Idempotent processing must prevent duplicate review events for retried requests.
The system must preserve template pinning, branding, checksum, and version metadata generated before review.
Review state changes must not bypass project and tenant boundary enforcement.
configurationRequired: RBAC permissions for Narratives and document review actions
Idempotency key retention configuration for POST operations
Allowed document review state transition map
Audit logging enablement and immutable log policy
loggingRequired: Log correlation id, tenant id, project id, document id, actor user id, requested action, previous review status, resulting review status, idempotency outcome, authorization outcome, If-Match/etag validation outcome, and persistence result; create immutable audit entries for every successful or failed sensitive state transition.
retryRequirement: Client retries are allowed only with the same idempotency key; server must ensure duplicate requests do not create duplicate review events or multiple status transitions. No automatic business retry after a validation or authorization failure.
transactionRequirement: The document status update, reviewer metadata update, document review event insert, and audit log insert must execute in a single transactional unit and roll back together on failure.

sampleInput.comments: Reviewed and approved for submission package inclusion.
